name: Independent Code Review
description: Changes are reviewed by an independent code reviewer prior to code being merged and released to production. Independent code review restricts the ability for anyone individual from taking a change production without involving an independent party (4-eyes principle).

attributes.text: Code Reviews are performed prior to committing a change to the main branch
testable_criteria: A code review record (e.g. pull request) exists for the change.
The code review was completed before the change was merged into the main branch (review timestamp precedes merge timestamp).
The change was not committed directly to the main branch without an associated code review.
The code review covers the actual code changes that were merged (no additional commits merged after review approval without further review).

attributes.text: Code Review approvals are performed by independent code reviewers
testable_criteria: The code review has at least one recorded approval.
The approver is a different individual from the author of the change (4-eyes principle).
The approver did not commit or co-author code on the change being reviewed.
The approver has authority/permissions to approve code reviews for the repository.

attributes.text: Testing is performed in accordance with the testing policy
testable_criteria: All unit tests executed for the change passed with zero failures.
All integration tests executed for the change passed.
End-to-end tests covering critical user journeys passed.
Performance tests passed where applicable.
Security tests/scans were executed and results were reviewed and approved.
Tests were executed in the CI/CD pipeline before merge.
Test results were visible in pull request status checks.
Unit test code coverage for new code is ≥ 80%.
Integration test coverage for critical integration points is ≥ 60%.
IF the change touches security-sensitive, payment, or authentication flows THEN test coverage for those paths is 100%.
IF the change modifies legacy code THEN coverage is maintained or improved versus the prior baseline.
Branch coverage is ≥ 70%.
Function coverage is ≥ 80%.
A coverage report was generated and reviewed as part of the code review.
No known critical or high-severity bugs are open against the change at release.
Manual smoke tests were completed for critical features (where applicable).

IF the change qualifies for an exception (legacy integration, third-party dependency, PoC/experimental, documentation-only, build/deployment scripts, dependency version bump, or behavior-preserving refactor) THEN the exception basis is documented on the change.
